answer:
verdict: fundable-ish but churn will scare VCs — fix that 1st

quick math:
- gross margin adj ARPU = $120 × 80% = $96/mo
- avg lifetime = 1/3% = ~33 mo
- LTV = $96 × 33 ≈ $3,200
- LTV:CAC = 4:1 ✅ (3+ is the bar)
- CAC payback = 800/96 ≈ 8.3 mo ✅ (under 12 is good)

so unit economics look solid on paper. BUT:

- 3% monthly logo churn = ~31% annual — thats SMB-tier & most B2B investors want <2%/mo, ideally <1%. theyll assume ur LTV is overstated bc churn usually worsens as u scale past early adopters
- logo churn ≠ revenue churn. if bigger accts stay & small ones leave, net revenue retention (NRR) could still be fine. investors care way more abt NRR — if urs is 100%+ ur story flips to strong
- $120 ARPU + $800 CAC = low-touch SMB motion, which only works w low churn or expansion revenue

what changes fundability:
1. get NRR ≥ 100% (upsells/expansion offset churn)
2. or cut logo churn to ~2%/mo
3. show cohort retention curves flattening — that alone can win the pitch

tldr: not dead, 4:1 LTV:CAC + 8mo payback gets meetings. churn is the objection ull hear every time, so walk in w cohort data + NRR
